page: Amazon Web Services d3.xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for workloads requiring massive local storage capacity. It features 5940 GB of local HDD storage, making it highly optimized for sequential data access and large-scale data retention. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the d3.xlarge is tailored for storage density rather than raw compute power. Its stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests, such as the read-only large block test at 26.91 GB/sec and the write large block test at 23.12 GB/sec, are positioned in the weak category. The instance achieves an average Geekbench single-core score of 1124.0, while its multi-core score of 2464.0 remains in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d3.xlarge offers high storage density relative to its compute resources, making it a cost-effective choice for massive data retention where high-speed SSD performance is not required. However, its weak performance in compute-heavy tasks, such as software compilation and LLM inference, represents a clear tradeoff. This instance is best suited for workloads such as distributed file systems, data warehousing, log processing, and large-scale data storage.
description: Amazon Web Services d3.xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 5940 GB of local HDD storage, 4 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance is designed for high-capacity storage workloads. Benchmark results show weak compute and memory bandwidth performance, with stress-ng and multi-core Geekbench scores falling in the bottom 25%. However, it maintains average single-core Geekbench and PassMark memory performance. The d3.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for massive data retention and sequential access workloads, trading off high-speed compute for dense, bundled local storage. It is best suited for distributed file systems, data warehousing, and log processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3.xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 5940 GB of local HDD storage, 4 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, optimized for high-capacity data workloads and sequential storage tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3.xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 5940 GB of local HDD storage, 4 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM.
tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for large-scale data retention and sequential workloads.
bullet_points: 5940 GB of bundled local HDD storage for high-capacity data retention.
Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM.
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 3 Gbps baseline network speed.
Optimized for sequential read/write operations and distributed file systems.
Trades off raw compute and memory bandwidth for high storage density.
